llm_reasoning: Crop suitability for tobacco is poor. The strongest positive factor is the zero frost risk, which is crucial for this sensitive crop. However, the mean annual temperature of 14.11°C is significantly below the optimal 20-30°C, severely limiting growth and yield. Annual rainfall (490.56 mm) is also critically low, necessitating full irrigation. Operational risks include insufficient heat units requiring early-maturing varieties or protected cultivation, and high irrigation costs. The slightly alkaline soil pH (7.17) may also cause nutrient deficiencies, requiring soil amendments or foliar feeding.
